target.title: fix(matrix): honor is_direct=false on bot member event as strict-DM veto
target.body:
Fixes #85017.

## Summary (revised after ClawSweeper review)

Updated scope to address ClawSweeper's review feedback. Three minimal patches across the three call sites that decide DM-vs-room routing, so an explicit `is_direct: false` on the bot's own `m.room.member` event vetoes strict-DM classification consistently:

1. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.ts`** — `inspectMatrixDirectRoomEvidence` folds `memberStateFlag === false` into `strict: false` so callers of `isStrictDirectRoom` (helper-level) immediately see the room as a group.

2. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.ts`** — `isDirectMessage`'s `m.direct`-account-data branch now consults `resolveDirectMemberFlag` and short-circuits to `false` when the local member state is explicit-false. Without this the inbound tracker still returned `true` for `m.direct`-mapped rooms before any local veto could fire — exactly the gap ClawSweeper flagged at `monitor/direct.ts:221`. The legacy "treats m.direct rooms as DMs" behavior is preserved when local state is `true` or absent.

3. **`extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.ts`** — `promoteMatrixDirectRoomCandidate` re-orders its checks so `memberStateFlag === false → reason: "local-explicit-false"` is matched before the generic `!strict → reason: "not-strict"`. Preserves the existing regression test contract at `direct-management.test.ts:281`. Without this, the helper-level change collapses both branches into `"not-strict"`, losing diagnostic value.

## Test coverage

- `direct-room.test.ts` — existing `records only the local member-state direct flag` test rewritten to assert the new veto (was encoding the buggy behavior). New companion `treats absent is_direct on the bot's member event as no signal` test pins the legacy count-based path so absent-`is_direct` rooms keep their existing DM classification.
- `monitor/direct.test.ts` — new `ignores m.direct mapping when local member state explicitly sets is_direct: false` test exercises the m.direct branch's new veto behavior. Existing `treats m.direct rooms as DMs` test continues to assert the absent-`is_direct` legacy path.
- `direct-management.test.ts` — no change required; the existing `does not classify rooms with local is_direct false as direct` test at line 281 continues to pass because the reason-ordering preserves `local-explicit-false`.

ClawSweeper's acceptance-criteria command:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \
  extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
```

## Scope NOT covered (deliberately, for a follow-up PR)

The bot's review noted that the broader fix would also veto strict-DM routing for **named rooms** (`m.room.name` present) and **rooms explicitly listed in `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>]`**, since those are also clear user-intent signals that the room is a group regardless of member count. Synapse's `/createRoom is_direct: false` does **not** propagate to member events — it only adds `is_direct: true` to invites, never `is_direct: false`. So rooms created with the explicit-false API don't actually carry the signal this PR honors; they need name-based or config-based vetoes to be fully covered.

Both of those vetoes touch additional config-plumbing surface (passing the channel's `groups` map into the classification path; reading and caching room name state) and felt out of scope for an issue-author PR that's already changing routing precedence. I've documented this gap on the issue thread. Happy to expand scope if maintainers prefer one larger PR, or land these three patches first and follow up.

## Real-environment evidence

Reproduction was verified end-to-end during the deployment that motivated #85017 (Unraid host running `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest`, `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` AIO Synapse, all over Tailscale tailnet). Sequence:

- Created `#parents` via `/createRoom` with `is_direct: false`, named, E2EE on, with the bot invited. The bot joins → 2 members.
- With `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [...] }` set, posted a **plain message (no `@openclaw` mention)**: bot replied. `requireMention` ignored — the bug.
- Added a 3rd user (`@admin`) → 3 members → `isStrictDirectMembership` returns `false` → re-posted plain message: bot ignored. Re-posted with `@openclaw` mention: bot replied in a thread (per channel-level `threadReplies: "always"`).
- Removed/re-introduced `dm.enabled`, moved per-room config between `rooms` and `groups`, set `requireMention: true` everywhere, etc. — none changed the behavior in 2-member rooms. The 3rd-member workaround is the only thing that made `requireMention` honored.

That confirms member-count is the sole gate today. The unit tests in this PR codify the explicit-`is_direct: false` veto path. For the layered name/config-based vetoes referenced in scope-not-covered, a maintainer can also use ClawSweeper Mantis to confirm live behavior after image rebuild.

## Related

- #57124 — earlier partial fix for "discovered DM candidates"; this PR layers the same intent onto the three runtime classification paths.
- #85017 — the filed issue with full reproduction.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: paulmeier
